title: Printer's Console Table
summary: A long console cabinet styled after antique printer's chests, with a bank of small faux drawer faces across two hinged doors.
description:
The look comes from a wall of small drawer faces, but only two of those openings are real doors behind them — the rest are fixed decorative fronts, which is exactly how the antique printer's cabinets this design borrows from were built too, since a real chest of that many individual drawers would have been impractical furniture. Build and hang the two working doors before attaching anything to the box; it's much easier to true up a door to its opening now than after the case is fully assembled.

Every face-frame and box joint gets a pocket hole drilled before assembly, hidden on the inside, underside, or top edges — plan those hole locations before any glue goes down, since a joint assembled first and drilled second usually means drilling blind into a corner you can no longer see into.

materials.name: Plywood, 3/4", 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Ripped into 15-3/4"-wide strips for the box.

cutList.part: Face frame top and bottom rails

steps.title: Build the face frames
steps.body: Build the face frames first, so you can be sure the door and drawer-face openings fit before anything else is built.

steps.title: Build the doors
steps.body: Build the two working doors to fit their openings and hang them with hinges, keeping them in place with catches for now. Do this before attaching anything else — it's much easier now than later. Keep track of which door goes in which opening.

steps.title: Drill the box pocket holes
steps.body: Before attaching any of the box pieces, drill 3/4" pocket holes on the front and back edges of every box piece, so the face frame can be attached easily in a later step. Hide these holes on the inside, underside, or top of the cabinet.

steps.title: Assemble the box
steps.body: Assemble the box from the top, bottom, and sides.

steps.title: Attach the face frame
steps.body: Attach the face frame to the box using the pocket holes drilled earlier.

steps.title: Add the decorative drawer faces
steps.body: Attach the decorative (non-functional) drawer faces across the remaining openings, trimmed with the 1x3 face trim.

steps.title: Add shelves and hardware
steps.body: Add the center divider and shelves inside the cabinet at the desired locations. Install knobs or handles on every drawer face, including the decorative ones, so the whole front reads consistently.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Attach the top boards and front trim to the cabinet.

steps.title: Add the back
steps.body:
Attach the back panel with glue and brad nails, if desired.

Sand every surface and finish.

images.alt: Printer's console table with a bank of decorative drawer faces
